# Spec context: §3.1 — Description of information fields

- Subsection: 3.1 Description of information fields

## Preceding prose

> Software that exports [abc tunes](#abc_tune_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") conforming to this standard should include a charset field if an encoding other than `utf-8` is used. All conforming abc software must be able to handle [text strings](#text_string_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") coded i
> Extensive information about [UTF-8](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UTF-8 "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UTF-8") and [ISO-8859](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO/IEC_8859 "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO/IEC_8859") can be found on wikipedia.
> Every [abc file](#abc_file_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") conforming to this standard should start with the line
> (see [abc file identification](#abc_file_identification "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵")).
> However to indicate tunes conforming to a different standard it is possible to use the `I:abc-version ` field, either in the [tune header](#tune_header_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") (for individual tunes) or in the [file header](#file_header_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> *Example:*
